text:
Xiaodong Yang
Xiaodong (Sheldon) Yang is an Assistant Professor of Practice in Operations Management and Business Analytics at NYU Shanghai. He holds a Ph.D. in Industrial and Operations Engineering from the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, as well as M.S. and B.S. degrees in Mechanical Engineering from Shanghai Jiao Tong University. His research focuses on the marketing-operations interface, and his work has been published in leading business journals, including Production and Operations Management and the Journal of Retailing.
Select Publications
“Partial Centralization in a Durable-Good Supply Chain”, Production and Operations Management, Vol. 32, No. 9, pp. 2775-2887, 2023. (with Li, J., V. Shi and G. Cai)

“Manufacturer-Retail Platform Interactions in The Presence of a Weak Retailer”, International Journal of Production Research, Vol. 57, No. 9, pp. 2732-2754, 2019. (with Shen, Y. and Y. Dai)
“Manufacturer-Retail Platform Interactions in The Presence of a Weak Retailer”, International Journal of Production Research, Vol. 57, No. 9, pp. 2732-2754, 2019. (with Shen, Y. and Y. Dai)

Xiaodong (Sheldon) Yang is an Assistant Professor of Practice in Operations Management and Business Analytics at NYU Shanghai. He holds a Ph.D. in Industrial and Operations Engineering from the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, as well as M.S. and B.S. degrees in Mechanical Engineering from Shanghai Jiao Tong University.
